So ladies and gentlemen, welcome to video number three inside of the clarity and understanding section of the DAP. In this section we're going to talk about self education. Now I didn't have any kind of educated background when it comes to college or even giving a fuck inside of high school. I just floated through it. I cheated on most of my tests and exams. It was really fun. My school actually implemented a system. Funny story is called the 90-10. 90% of my grade came from the tests that we would do and 10% of the weight behind a sign language. It was made up of like homework and literally all other work. So I schemed the system. I actually didn't do literally any work at all throughout my entire junior and senior year. Besides tests, because guess what? If I failed the test, the teacher would have to educate me on the one-to-one level to then show me what I needed to do exactly to pass the test. At which point I actually went through junior and senior year with a 3.5 or higher senior year being 4.0 because I schemed the system. I gained the game all because I chose the path of self education. I was self-aware enough to gain the system. When you're self-aware enough to do things, ladies and gentlemen, you can take over life. You can crush it in areas where other people's can't because you choose to self-educate. Each day I choose to read one book. So I read a book a day. Now as soon as you hear that, you might be like, Jeremy, there's no way to read a book a day, dude. You're absolutely right. There is no way I'm reading a full book a day. And you'd be crazy to think the same thing. You'd actually overwhelm yourself in a point where you probably would be like, reading is crazy. And people would think they can read a book a day, but no, they don't. Those are scammers. Okay, well, check this out. Dummy, check it out. Look, you read one chapter a day. That's it. One chapter. Go in. Read two chapters. Read three. Get excited about the book through something that educates you and makes you one percent better in a day. Because check this out. If you do one thing each day for 365 days and you look at that on a percentage basis, you are going to be one percent better today for tomorrow. And if 365 days pass, you're 365 percent better than you were the prior year. That compounds over time. If you guys have ever seen the Warren Bluffet documentary, which I highly recommend, I would go out and tell you about compounding. And I would tell you that it is one of the greatest things you could possibly invest your time into. Self-educating yourself. Warren Bluffet reads five to six hours a day. If you think you're better than Warren Bluffet, you don't read. Now, that's great. If you're a hidden Warren Bluffet that nobody knows about yet, you're worth more than 70-something billion dollars. And maybe you also came from nothing and grew past that point. Maybe you're the documentary that you need to send a link for. But if you're not somebody who came from absolutely nothing and made 70-something billion dollars, and you say that it's not smart to read five to six hours a day like Warren Bluffet does, I don't really know where you're coming from. I'm just trying to repeat successful actions. So I do. I pick up all these different books. What type of books do you want to read?
